text:
Market today: Iran deal headlines are driving a risk-on mood — the 'Monday rally after weekend strikes' pattern is real and working again. Lower geopolitical fear = money flows back into growth and tech, which helps your book broadly.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 14.66% and your largest position. On margin, that hole is already painful and getting worse before it stabilizes. Watch the $336 stop hard. PLTR is down 11.08% with no clear catalyst to reverse it right now — stop at $85 is far away, so you have room, but monitor closely. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — even a 6.62% gain can evaporate fast; margin makes this especially dangerous if it whips back.

Watch: 1) Does AVGO show any stabilization or does selling continue — that's your margin risk epicenter. 2) Broader tech/chip sentiment via SMH, your one green chip name right now.

Bottom line: Protect AVGO first — it's your largest position, it's losing, and margin means the pain compounds fast.
